title: unwrap() in WalletMasterKey methods missing required comment
description:
Scenario: a contributor reads CLAUDE.md §11 ('Any new unwrap() in a production code path requires a comment') and audits the codebase. They find two undocumented unwrap() calls in core/src/wallet.rs:

  in from_seed(): bytes[..32].try_into().unwrap() and bytes[32..].try_into().unwrap()
  in from_bytes(): same pattern

CLAUDE.md explicitly requires these be commented. The unwrap()s are safe (slicing a 64-byte hash with fixed offsets cannot fail), but the comment must say so.

Maintenance consequence: establishes a precedent that the unwrap rule in CLAUDE.md is not enforced. Future contributors add undocumented unwrap()s in genuinely fallible paths citing these as examples.

Fix: add comment before each:
  // Slicing known-length BLAKE2b-512 output with fixed offsets — cannot fail.
